summary: Optimize rust-diffforge terminal startup, resize batching, WebGL attachment, and telemetry logging without changing terminal contracts.

review_items.summary: Cache agent command candidate resolution so repeated terminal opens do not repeatedly run npm global-prefix discovery.
Move terminal fit scheduling to a workspace-level resize coordinator that observes pane containers and flushes native resize batches once per animation frame.
Defer and stagger xterm WebGL renderer attachment so terminal startup and first output are not blocked by every pane attaching WebGL immediately.
Serialize terminal telemetry writes through a shared native writer and batch frontend telemetry events to prevent corrupt JSONL output during resize storms.
Add frontend telemetry for workspace activation, workspace surface commits, and terminal mount timing so pre-agent workspace open delays can be measured.
Add frontend render diagnostics for xterm DOM, canvas, buffer, cursor, and viewport state so blank WebGL panes can be diagnosed without logging terminal text.
Detect terminal startups that only receive a tiny control chunk and never paint visible buffer rows, then refresh WebGL and retry the pane with capped backoff.
Pace queued terminal opens slightly so many Codex processes do not all initialize at the same instant.
Detach native close-all cleanup so app close cannot drain PTY state and leave the WebView stuck with stale terminal panes.
Shorten the post-auth workspace ready gate because telemetry shows workspace API loading finishes quickly and the fixed 1800 ms wait dominates the first open.
Measure agent status startup and run npm package metadata checks in parallel with provider runtime checks so terminal readiness is not serialized behind npm metadata.
Skip disposed terminal spawn queue entries before applying spawn pacing and defer dev-only terminal startup slightly so React StrictMode cleanup does not occupy the native spawn queue.
Start npm version, npm package metadata, and provider runtime checks together so agent readiness is not blocked by npm metadata sequencing.
Use an 800 ms blank-startup check/retry cadence and an 800 ms terminal spawn gap to keep retries and terminal launches from overlapping.
Use a shorter initial terminal spawn gap and reserve the 800 ms gap for retry launches after telemetry showed the global 800 ms gap made workspace startup visibly laggier.
Treat the 800 ms blank-startup check as a probe and require a second 800 ms confirmation before restarting, reducing false retry churn while keeping retry attempts close together.
Cache non-secret local agent readiness booleans and versions so known-good agents can render the workspace while fresh agent status checks run in the background.

Run fresh agent status and npm update availability checks during authenticated workspace loading, prompt users to update available npm-installed agents before entering, and auto-open agent settings when no ready agent is available.

rules: Terminal pane ids, instance id stale-checks, and PTY size validation remain enforced.
The terminal_open, terminal_resize_many, terminal_write, terminal_close, and terminal_close_all command contracts remain backward compatible.
Telemetry changes may reduce hot-path IPC volume but must preserve phase names needed to diagnose startup and resizing.
Terminal render diagnostics must not log raw terminal output, prompts, commands, or user-entered text.
Blank-startup recovery may retry a pane only with a low cap, an 800 ms probe/confirm cadence, and must keep WebGL enabled.
Exited or missing native PTY sessions must stop receiving resize requests from the frontend.
WebGL remains best-effort and falls back to the default xterm renderer when unavailable.
Agent package updates must remain user-triggered and must not auto-update before workspace entry without an explicit click.
No auth, billing, database, or cross-project API contract changes are introduced.

compatibility.notes: The changes are internal to rust-diffforge terminal runtime behavior and keep existing Tauri command names and request shapes.

tests.summary: Validate Tauri commands and Rust host code.
